mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals a breaking labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, causing equity markets to price in recession risks and deliver negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in real personal income growth compresses household purchasing power, triggering consumer spending cuts that reduce corporate revenue expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.5% monthly while RSAFS growth turns negative, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals resilient manufacturing output that offsets rate hike concerns, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets reprice growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to correlate with positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, with a rolling 1-year Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained housing starts above 1.5 million units annualized indicate strong construction demand and wealth effects that boost consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as corporate earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of months where HOUST remains above the 1.5 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation accelerates beyond 0.6% monthly while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in greater than 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized monthly expansion in industrial production and total nonfarm payrolls reflects broad-based economic strength that supports corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors rotate into cyclical equities.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive skew or Rank IC below 0.01 when both INDPRO and PAYEMS register positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter rise in unemployment exceeding 0.2 percentage points coinciding with flat or declining real personal income signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2 percentage points quarter-over-quarter
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 50% of quarters where the unemployment threshold is breached alongside stagnant real income growth.
